span.p:
Anchor selection
5 judges × combined-eval call
scores ambiguity feasibility per type

div.style: color: rgb(0, 0, 0) !important; display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Stage 1 · Perturbation generation
3 SOTA models concurrent
(gpt-5.4 · claude-sonnet · gemini-3.1-pro)
──────────
v2 reform B: opt-out when no natural ambiguity
v2 reform C: info-conservation rules

div.style: color: rgb(0, 0, 0) !important; display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Per generator returns one of:
• perturbed_prompt + interp_a + interp_b
• null + opt-out reason
• error

div.style: color: rgb(0, 0, 0) !important; display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Stage 1.5 · Quality gate (NEW v2 reform D)
1 judge × 3 binary checks:
• leakage (info added beyond clean?)
• B-naturalness (would a programmer write B?)
• distinguishability (A and B differ on common inputs?)

div.style: color: rgb(0, 0, 0) !important; display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;
color: rgb(0, 0, 0) !important; display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;
color: rgb(0, 0, 0) !important; display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Stage 2 · Bilateral naturalness gate
5 judges × 2 yes/no questions:
Q_A: is interpretation A natural?
Q_B: is interpretation B natural?
──────────
v2 reform A: both yes_a ≥ 3 AND yes_b ≥ 3
(replaces v1 single-choice entropy gate)

div.style: color: rgb(0, 0, 0) !important; display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;
color: rgb(0, 0, 0) !important; display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;
color: rgb(0, 0, 0) !important; display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Stage 3 · Test_b generation
same SOTA model that wrote the perturbation
writes ref_solution_b + test_b
(DS-1000 → self-contained code)

div.style: color: rgb(0, 0, 0) !important; display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Stage 4 · Sandbox exclusivity gate
Docker, no LLM calls
──────────
2 × 2 matrix must satisfy:
ref_a × test_a → ✓ pass
ref_a × test_b → ✗ fail
ref_b × test_a → ✗ fail
ref_b × test_b → ✓ pass
